blurb: Small open rosettes with pale butter-green centres that wash out to cream and flush rose-red along the finely toothed, hair-fringed edges. Branches up into a little shrub of colour.
size: Rosettes ~2–3 in, branching to a small shrub

care.light: Bright light with some gentle sun brings out the yellow-and-rose colour; harsh mid-summer glare scorches the thin leaves, and deep shade turns it plain green and leggy.
care.water: Soak-and-dry while it's actively growing in the cooler half of the year; keep it much drier through its summer rest.
care.soil: A gritty, fast-draining mix — those fibrous roots dislike staying wet.
care.temperature: Comfortable at room temperature; keep it above ~30°F and out of frost.
care.dormancy: A winter grower: it does most of its growing in the cool, bright months and rests (often shedding lower leaves and closing its rosettes) in summer heat — ease right off water then.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice during the cool growing season.
care.potting: Repot in autumn as growth restarts; it branches readily, so give it room to bush out.
care.propagation: Stem-tip cuttings root easily — let the cut callus a day or two, then set in dry mix.
care.bloom: Rosettes are monocarpic — an individual rosette that flowers then dies, but the branching plant carries on from its other heads.
care.troubles: Summer overwatering rot, scorch in harsh sun, and legginess in low light; watch for aphids on new growth.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic and considered safe around cats and dogs.
